arguments.task: Visit the official Waterfall.io website and official first-party pages only. Determine whether Waterfall.io offers a public API, developer documentation, authentication methods, pricing or plan gating, and any official MCP or model context protocol server. Prefer canonical pages linked from the site such as product, docs, API, integrations, security, support, or pricing pages. Open pages and read their content. Return concise findings with the exact live URLs visited that support: one-line product description, auth methods for any API, access tier/gating, API surface type and breadth, MCP existence, buildability verdict, rate limit notes, and notable signals. If docs are missing, thin, JS-rendered without useful content, or gated, say so explicitly.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect official Waterfall.io pages and extract supported evidence from live pages.

raw_result.description: Data enrichment and prospecting platform with APIs to find, enrich, and verify contacts and companies.
raw_result.auth_notes: The authentication docs specify sending an API key in the x-api-key HTTP header to access https://api.waterfall.io/v1/. No other first-party API auth method was evidenced in the gathered pages.
raw_result.gating_reason: The homepage states usage-based access with 'Pay only for what you actually use' and 'No monthly commitments,' indicating self-serve onboarding rather than partner-only gating; however, specific signup/trial mechanics were not evidenced in the gathered pages.

api_surface.breadth: Documented REST API with enrichment and prospecting endpoints, including async job-based flows for contact, company, phone, and reveal enrichment, plus synchronous email verification and usage tracking; docs mention an OpenAPI spec.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP or Model Context Protocol server was found in the official homepage, docs pages, or docs llms.txt index gathered in this pass.

raw_result.blocker: Public API docs are available, but the gathered evidence does not directly confirm immediate self-serve API key issuance from a signup flow, so initial access may require manual account setup.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The rate-limit docs describe X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Interval headers, with HTTP 429 and Retry-After when limits are exceeded.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are substantial and include an llms.txt index; the product emphasizes a multi-provider 'waterfall' approach and usage tracking/cost visibility in API responses.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The evidence supports the API type and auth method, but it does not directly show whether API access is immediately self-serve after signup versus requiring account approval/manual provisioning, so access_tier and buildability_verdict remain somewhat uncertain.
